title: New Bakstre
content:

{{Quote|The Coalition's approach is considerably more restrained than similar sentiments expressed by the more radical Mechanical Liberation Front, who attempted to 'free' a shipment of Vindicator XM-15s and ended up destroying a 25-kilometer radius of Havridam City on New Bakstre last month, killing all 14 activists.|From "Droid Rights Activists Decry Brilliant Missiles," HoloNet News, Volume 51|<ref name="HNN" />}}
'''New Bakstre''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located within the [[Tendrannan sector/Legends|Tendrannan subsector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim's]] [[New Territories/Legends|New Territories]]. Part of the loosely affiliated domain known as the [[Cassandran Worlds/Legends|Cassandran Worlds]], New Bakstre was a planet dominated by industry situated on the important [[Braxant Run/Legends|Braxant Run]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] that served much of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] northern quadrant. One of New Bakstre's major [[City/Legends|settlements]], [[Havridam City]], was partially destroyed in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] after activists from the [[Mechanical Liberation Front]] accidentally detonated a shipment of [[Vindicator XM-15 "brilliant" missile]]s. The planet was involved in the [[Outer Rim Sieges/Legends|later stages]] of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] when [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]-led [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|forces]] [[Battle of New Bakstre|engaged]] the [[Confederacy military/Legends|military]] of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] on the world's surface. [[Annexation of the Cassandran Worlds|Annexed]] by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] along with the entirety of the Cassandran Worlds roughly twenty [[Standard year/Legends|years]] later, New Bakstre remained under Imperial control during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] and the [[Second Galactic Civil War]], with a brief intermediate spell, during which it was allied with the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. New Bakstre possessed seven [[Moon/Legends|moons]], a rapid rotation rate, and a sky that was [[Color/Legends|colored]] purple.

==Description==
New Bakstre was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="TRG1-17" /> located in the [[New Bakstre system/Legends|system of the same name]]. Situated in the [[New Territories/Legends|New Territories]] region<ref name="TEA" /> of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]],<ref name="EAOC" /> New Bakstre sat between the planets of [[Cassander]] and [[Isiring]] on the [[Braxant Run/Legends|Braxant Run]], the most important [[Hyperspace route/Legends|trade route]] in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] northern quadrant that ran from the planet of [[Bandomeer/Legends|Bandomeer]] through several [[Sector/Legends|sectors]] to out past [[Xerton Nine]].<ref name="TEA" /> New Bakstre was originally part of the domain known as the [[Cassandran Worlds/Legends|Cassandran Worlds]],<ref name="CSWE-CW">{{CSWECite|I|111|Cassandran Worlds}}</ref> a loose alliance of more than a hundred [[Star system/Legends|star systems]]. Shortly before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] which took place in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] took control of the Cassandran Worlds, splitting the region into three separate sectors known as the [[Cassandran Worlds/Legends|Cassandran sectors]]—[[Cassander subsector|Cassander]], [[Tadrin sector/Legends|Tadrin]], and [[Tendrannan sector/Legends|Tendrannan]]. New Bakstre fell into the newly created Tendrannan subsector.<ref name="GATORW" />

[[Orbit/Legends|Orbiting]] a single [[Star/Legends|star]], New Bakstre was in turn circled by seven [[Moon/Legends|moons]].<ref name="TRG1-17" /> The planet had a rapid rate of rotation,<ref name="SWE">''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> and its sky was [[Color/Legends|purple]]-tinted when viewed from the surface.<ref name="TRG1-17" /> The [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] was breathable to [[Human/Legends|Humans]],<ref name="Atmos" /> and the native flora included [[Tree/Legends|forests]] and [[twisted vegetation]].<ref name="TRG1-17" /> One of its major [[City/Legends|population centers]] was [[Havridam City]].<ref name="HNN" /> By [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], New Bakstre was home to between 100 and 500 million inhabitants.<ref name="TEA" />

==History==
{{Quote|General Traavis fought throughout the five months of the Outer Rim Sieges, moving troopers, warships and commandos to battlefields on the Cassandran Worlds of Monhudle, New Bakstre, and Biitu.|From Incident Report #890-G, filed by [[Clone commando/Legends|clone commando]] [[Sarge (clone commando)|Sarge]], [[Aiwha Squad]]|<ref name="Order66">{{InsiderCite|87|Order 66: Destroy All Jedi}}</ref>}}
Around a [[Standard century/Legends|century]] prior to the fall of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], an [[Esselian]] [[Esselian conglomerate|conglomerate]] headed by businessman [[Cassander Tadrin]] invested in a large exploration project, sending [[Survey droid/Legends|survey droids]] into unexplored areas of the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]. The undertaking was a success, discovering several planets in an uncharted section of the New Territories in the galaxy's northern quadrant. Bored and dissatisfied with the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]' culture of self-satisfaction, Tadrin sold his company holdings and took tens of thousands of his loyal employees and their families to settle his new discoveries. The worlds became a loosely united region of more than a hundred star systems called the Cassandran Worlds, governed from the planet of Cassander, then known formally as "Cassander's Star." New Bakstre was one of the region's industrial powerhouses alongside [[Minashee/Legends|Minashee]].<ref name="GATORW" />

In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], the [[Mechanical Liberation Front]], a group of [[Droid/Legends|droid]] rights activists, attempted to free a shipment of [[Vindicator XM-15 "brilliant" missile]]s near Havridam City on New Bakstre. They believed that the [[Arakyd Industries/Legends|Arakyd Industries]]–manufactured [[Missile/Legends|missiles]]' [[Central processor/Legends|central processor]] was so advanced that the resulting weapon should be considered as a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] being doomed to live an explosive existence.<ref name="HNN" /> The Mechanical Liberation Front's efforts set off the missiles' explosive [[Warhead/Legends|warheads]],<ref name="CSWE-HC">{{CSWECite|II|26|Havridam City}}</ref> which caused the destruction of a twenty-five-[[Kilometer/Legends|kilometer]] area of Havridam City, and [[Death/Legends|killed]] all fourteen activists conducting the raid.<ref name="HNN" />

During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], fought between the Republic and the breakaway [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], New Bakstre was located within Separatist-controlled [[Realspace/Legends|space]].<ref name="TEA" /> Toward the end of the war, during the [[Outer Rim Sieges/Legends|Outer Rim Sieges]], Republic [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|forces]]—which included [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]], [[Clone commando/Legends|commandos]], and [[Warship/Legends|warships]]—led by the [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]] [[Traavis]] [[Battle of New Bakstre|fought]] Separatist forces on New Bakstre and throughout the Cassandran Worlds, including on [[Monhudle]] and [[Biitu]].<ref name="Order66" /> The Republic claimed victory in the Clone Wars, and [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] shortly thereafter [[Declaration of a New Order|reorganized]] it into the Galactic Empire, declaring himself [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]].<ref name="RotS">{{Film|III}}</ref>

[[File:Cassander_region.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The Cassandran Worlds and their surrounding region]]
Shortly before the Battle of [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin]] in 0 BBY, an Imperial [[Battle group/Legends|task force]] arrived at Cassander and [[Annexation of the Cassandran Worlds|annexed]] the Cassandran Worlds, dividing the region into three distinct subsectors: the Cassander subsector, administered from Cassander itself; the Tadrin subsector, ruled from Minashee; and the Tendrannan subsector, governed from Isiring.<ref name="GATORW" /> New Bakstre was located within the bounds of the Tendrannan subsector.<ref name="EAOC" /> Along with a swath of the Outer Rim Territories, New Bakstre came under the authority of the Empire's [[Oversector Outer]], ruled over by [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]] and administered from the planet [[Eriadu/Legends|Eriadu]]<ref name="TEA" /> in the [[Seswenna sector/Legends|Seswenna sector]].<ref name="EAOC" /> Following Tarkin's death at the Battle of Yavin, command of [[Oversector/Legends|Oversector]] Outer was passed to Grand Moff [[Ardus Kaine]], who moved the administration center for the region to the planet [[Entralla/Legends|Entralla]]<ref name="TEA" /> in the [[Velcar sector/Legends|Velcar sector]].<ref name="EAOC" />

{{RPGseed}}
During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] fought between the Empire and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], [[Imperial Office of Customs/Legends|Imperial Customs]] [[Cruiser/Legends|cruisers]] patrolled New Bakstre. At some point, they forced down a [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]], the ''[[Dorion Discus]]'', [[Pilot/Legends|piloted]] by the [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] [[Roark Garnet/Legends|Roark Garnet]] and [[Hawk Carrow]], who were attempting to make an unscheduled delivery to the world. The [[Starship/Legends|starship]] crashed into a forest, and Imperial [[Soldier/Legends|troops]] wounded and captured Carrow, taking him to the [[Medical bay/Legends|infirmary]] aboard the [[M-Class Imperial Attack Transport]] ''[[Regina Cayli]]''. Garnet subsequently infiltrated the ship and rescued his friend.<ref name="TRG1-17" />
{{Endgame}}

[[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] Palpatine perished during the Alliance victory at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], triggering a widespread collapse of Imperial authority across the galaxy as ambitious [[Moff/Legends|moffs]] and military officials took the opportunity to form their own fiefdoms. Grand Moff Kaine reduced the size of Oversector Outer so that it only encompassed the northern quadrant centered around the New Territories, effectively protecting his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Bastion/Legends|Sartinaynian]]. New Bakstre and the Cassandran Worlds remained within space that Kaine controlled from 4 ABY until [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]]. During that [[Time/Legends|time]], Oversector Outer was renamed and reorganized into the [[Pentastar Alignment]], a [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] dominion. Kaine was killed during [[Operation Shadow Hand]], the campaign that the resurrected Emperor Palpatine instigated in [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]] to topple the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], the successor state to the Alliance. The Alignment foundered without Kaine, until [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]] took control of the region in 12 ABY and incorporated it into the heart of his restructured [[Imperial Remnant|Empire]].<ref name="TEA" /> The Empire lightly defended the Cassandran Worlds, with only a pair of [[Victory-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyers]] and a few [[Flight group/Legends|flights]] of [[TIE series/Legends|TIE Series]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] in the region.<ref name="GATORW" /> New Bakstre remained under Imperial control until [[17 ABY/Legends|17 ABY]],<ref name="TEA" /> when the Empire was forced to retreat into eight sectors under the pressure of a [[Imperial Skirmishes|concentrated New Republic campaign]].<ref name="EGTW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref>

Following the Empire's retreat from the Cassandran Worlds, the region joined the New Republic. The association was short-lived, however, as the domain was sundered by the [[Yuuzhan Vong War|advance]] of the [[Extragalactic/Legends|extra-galactic]] invaders known as the [[Yuuzhan Vong]], who waged war on the galaxy<ref name="GATORW" /> from 25 ABY to [[29 ABY/Legends|29 ABY]]. By the time of the [[Second Galactic Civil War]], contested between the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances]]—the New Republic's successor—and the breakaway [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]]-led [[Confederation]], New Bakstre was once again located in Imperial-held territory. By [[137 ABY]], New Bakstre was situated within the borders of Emperor [[Roan Fel]]'s [[Empire-in-exile]].<ref name="TEA" />

==Locations==
One of New Bakstre's major urban settlements was Havridam City.<ref name="CSWE-HC" /> In 22 BBY, a large section of the city was destroyed when activists from the Mechanical Liberation Front attempted to "rescue" a shipment of Vindicator XM-15 "brilliant" missiles.<ref name="HNN" />

==Behind the scenes==
New Bakstre was the principal setting of [[Regina Cayli (adventure)|''Regina Cayli'']], an adventure included in [[West End Games]]' [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game (first edition)|''Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game'']], published in [[1987]]. The adventure was subsequently reprinted in [[1994]]'s [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 1|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 1]]. New Bakstre received an entry in [[1998]]'s ''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''. In [[2002]], the planet was referenced in [[HoloNet News Vol. 531 51]], an online series of [[in-universe]] news reports leading up to the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|start]] of the Clone Wars.

''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]'', published by [[Wizards of the Coast]] in 2002, included a mention of New Bakstre and its links to the Cassandran Worlds in the sourcebook's entry for the planet [[Garqi/Legends|Garqi]]. The [[Star Wars Insider 87|eighty-seventh]] issue of ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'', published in [[2006]], referenced New Bakstre in [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[General/Legends|General]] Traavis' entry in the article ''[[Order 66: Destroy All Jedi]]''. [[2008]]'s ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' included an entry for New Bakstre, and it was also mentioned in the book's entries for the Cassandran Worlds and Havridam City. [[2009]]'s ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' and its [[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion|online companion]] made reference to New Bakstre and provided details on its astrographical location in the galaxy.

''Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds'' states that the Yuuzhan Vong "ravaged" the Cassandran Worlds during their war on the galaxy, which is also supported by several maps in ''The Essential Atlas'', which depict many known Cassandran Worlds under Yuuzhan Vong control. However, New Bakstre is never specifically stated to have been attacked or captured by the invaders.
